candidate_mechanism.title: Minimal Plasticity Core

equations.feature_core: f_{t+1} = (1 - l_f) * f_t + e_f * q_t * (L_t + k_a * A_t * L_t - k_i * I_t)
equations.coding_state: y_t = W_f * f_t + W_A * vec(A_t) + W_m * m_t

interpretation.phase_core: one gate decides whether update mass goes to feature growth or topology consolidation
interpretation.feature_core: feature generation only keeps local contrast, routed reuse, and interference suppression
interpretation.structure_core: structure formation only keeps feature coactivation, demand mismatch, and prune pressure
interpretation.memory_core: slow memory remains the only stabilizer and pruning anchor
interpretation.core_claim: The minimal plasticity core only needs four mechanisms: phase gating, local contrast extraction, demand-shaped structure growth, and slow stabilization.

candidate_mechanism.core_claim: The minimal plasticity core only needs four mechanisms: phase gating, local contrast extraction, demand-shaped structure growth, and slow stabilization.

project_readout.summary: P7B is positive only if the co-evolution equation can be compressed into a smaller plasticity core without losing too much signal, residual control, or brain plausibility.
project_readout.next_question: If P7B holds, the next stage should push high-risk brain-side falsifiers directly against this minimal core.
